relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Trust is the substance of hope and conviction of unseen things.
By trust the elders received witness.
By trust we perceive ages shaped by Divine word.
Abel still speaks through trusted offering.
Enoch is carried by a life pleasing to Divine.
Drawing near rests on trust that Divine is and rewards seekers.
Noah prepares refuge for his household by reverent trust.
Abraham goes without knowing the destination.
He sojourns in promise-land as foreigner.
He awaits the city built by Divine.
Sarah receives power because the Promiser is trustworthy.
From seeming deadness comes star-like multitude.
They die seeing promises from afar and confessing sojourner life.
Such confession seeks homeland.
Return was possible, but desire moved elsewhere.
They desire the better homeland, and Divine prepares a city.
Abraham offers Isaac in testing.
The promise remains named through Isaac.
Abraham reckons Divine able to raise from death.
Isaac blesses toward coming things.
Jacob blesses and worships at life's edge.
Joseph remembers exodus and bones.
Moses' parents hide beauty without fear.
Moses refuses Pharaoh's household identity.
He chooses hardship with Divine people over temporary misalignment.
He sees greater wealth in reproach than Egypt's treasures.
He endures by seeing the unseen One.
Passover is kept by trust.
The sea is crossed as dry land.
Walls fall after faithful encircling.
Rahab receives the scouts in peace.
More witnesses overflow the telling.
Trust works justice and receives promises.
Weakness is empowered and danger endured.
Women receive their dead; others wait for better resurrection.
Others endure public shame and imprisonment.
Some wander deprived and mistreated.
The world-system was not worthy of them.
All are witnessed through trust without yet receiving completion.
Divine prepared completion together with us.

contemplative_note: Hebrews 11 is the ancestral river of trust: wandering, blessing, resisting, welcoming, enduring, and seeing the unseen city.
